title: Iterable unpacking in for-loops and yield from should use an iterable object
description: Avoid using iterable unpacking in for-loops and yield from with non-iterable objects because it can lead to runtime errors.
message: Iterable unpacking in for-loops and yield from should use an iterable object
recommendation: Use an iterable object for iterable unpacking in for-loops and yield from

noncompliant.description: Iterable unpacking with a single-element tuple

noncompliant.code: for a, b in (1, None): pass
noncompliant.description: Iterable unpacking with a non-iterable object

compliant.code: for a, b in iter([1, 2, 3]): pass
compliant.description: Iterable unpacking with an iter() call
Iterable unpacking with iter() and an empty tail

compliant.code: for a, b in itertools.chain([1, 2, 3], [4, 5, 6]): pass
for a, b in itertools.islice((1, 2, 3, 4, 5), 3): pass
for a, b in iter(itertools.cycle([1, 2, 3]), None): pass
compliant.description: Iterable unpacking with itertools.cycle() and a stop iterator

why_is_this_an_issue: Non-iterable objects can cause runtime errors when used with iterable unpacking
how_to_fix: Use an iterable object for iterable unpacking in for-loops and yield from

metadata.title: Iterable unpacking in for-loops and yield from should use an iterable object
